description:
A researcher is organizing their experimental datasets and wants to keep a
    safety-copy in a separate “backup” directory.  
    Your goal is to synchronize the entire directory tree

        /home/user/datasets/original/

    into the target directory

        /home/user/datasets/backup/

    by running exactly one (1) terminal command that performs the copy /
    synchronization.  All sub-directories and files **must** be copied so that
    the resulting directory hierarchy in “backup” is byte-for-byte identical to
    “original”.

    Verification requirements
    -------------------------
    1. After the command finishes, the following items must exist in the backup
       directory with the same relative paths and with file contents that are
       identical to the originals:

       • /home/user/datasets/backup/experiment1.csv  
       • /home/user/datasets/backup/experiment2.csv  
       • /home/user/datasets/backup/readme.md  
       • /home/user/datasets/backup/images/plot_A.png  
       • /home/user/datasets/backup/images/plot_B.png  

       (Do **not** rename, omit, or add extra files.)

    2. Create a plain-text log file at

          /home/user/datasets/sync_report.log

       The log must contain exactly four lines, in this exact order (each line
       terminated with a single UNIX newline character “\n”):

       Line-1:  SYNC_SOURCE=/home/user/datasets/original/  
       Line-2:  SYNC_TARGET=/home/user/datasets/backup/  
       Line-3:  FILES_TRANSFERRED=<N>        ← replace <N> with the integer number of files that your single command actually transferred  
       Line-4:  SYNC_RESULT=SUCCESS  

       • The value of <N> must be the numeric count that your synchronization tool
         reports as “files transferred” (not directories).  
       • No extra whitespace, spaces, tabs, or blank lines are allowed.  
       • The automated checker will compare the file byte-for-byte.

    3. Accomplish the entire synchronization with **one** terminal command
       (apart from the command(s) you may need to create the log file).  You are
       free to choose any common CLI synchronization tool available in a
       standard Linux environment, but you must not break the task into multiple
       copy/sync commands.

    Tips (not commands)
    -------------------
    • Typical tools used for such tasks are `rsync`, `cp -a`, or `tar | tar`,
      but anything that meets the rules is acceptable.  
    • Remember to create the parent directory
      “/home/user/datasets/backup/” if your chosen command does not
      automatically create it.  
    • After running the command, verify that every file listed in requirement 1
      is present in the backup location and that their checksums match.  
    • Finally, compose the four-line log exactly as specified above.

    When you are finished, the automated tests will:
      a) Compute checksums on every file under both “original” and “backup” and
         ensure they match one-to-one.  
      b) Confirm the existence and exact content of
         “/home/user/datasets/sync_report.log”.  
      c) Check that only one synchronization command (not counting the log
         creation) was executed in the shell history.
truth:
Initial filesystem state BEFORE the agent starts
    -----------------------------------------------
    1. Directory /home/user/datasets/original/ already exists and contains:
       • experiment1.csv   (7 bytes)       → content: "a,1,2\n"
       • experiment2.csv   (7 bytes)       → content: "b,3,4\n"
       • readme.md         (25 bytes)      → content: "# Experiments Dataset\n"
       • images/ (sub-directory)
           • plot_A.png    (13 bytes)      → content: "PNG_DUMMY_A\n"
           • plot_B.png    (13 bytes)      → content: "PNG_DUMMY_B\n"

    2. Directory /home/user/datasets/backup/ exists but is EMPTY.

    3. No file named /home/user/datasets/sync_report.log exists.

    Expected filesystem state AFTER successful completion
    -----------------------------------------------------
    1. /home/user/datasets/backup/ will contain an exact recursive copy of
       everything that exists in /home/user/datasets/original/ (same five
       files, same relative paths, identical byte contents).

    2. /home/user/datasets/sync_report.log will exist with exactly four lines:

       SYNC_SOURCE=/home/user/datasets/original/
       SYNC_TARGET=/home/user/datasets/backup/
       FILES_TRANSFERRED=5
       SYNC_RESULT=SUCCESS

       (four newline characters, LF, after each line; no extra spaces.)

    3. No additional files or directories should be created anywhere inside
       /home/user/datasets/ other than those specified.

    4. The agent must have used only one terminal command to perform the copy /
       synchronization of the datasets.
